consensus_findings.description: D5 / task 1.1 claims to update the tooling-roadmap spec's Purpose field (currently 'TBD - created by archiving change roadmap-v2-perplexity-integration. Update Purpose after archive.'), but the delta file at openspec/changes/roadmap-v2-reconciliation/specs/tooling-roadmap/spec.md contains only a '## MODIFIED Requirements' section. OpenSpec's archive tooling only applies requirement-level ADDED/MODIFIED/REMOVED deltas; it has no mechanism to update the Purpose section from a delta file. Evidence: 5+ synced specs in openspec/specs/ carry 'TBD' Purpose placeholders that survived archival (delegation-spawner, role-registry, extension-registry, cli-interface, tooling-roadmap). Under the current plan, D5 will silently not happen at archive time.

The new 'Roadmap Document Authoritative' requirement defines 'phase change' tautologically via the roadmap table (per design D1's reference-based approach) but the scenario preamble uses examples only: 'a new capability introduction, a bootstrap-v4.1 P-item, a perplexity §8 item'. For ambiguous cases — e.g., a substantial hygiene change, a pure refactor with its own spec delta, or a spec-sync reconciling a phase — authors lack a decision rule. Design D4 explicitly acknowledges this by labeling non-phase listing a 'permission, not a mandate', but that guideline lives only in design.md, not in the spec itself, so future authors reading the spec alone cannot decide consistently.

D4 ('spec-sync CAN be a phase — permission not mandate') creates authoring ambiguity without a default. The same kind of change (spec-sync reconciling a phase's validation output) could land with or without a roadmap row depending on the author. This undercuts the 'single authoritative registry' property the capability is trying to preserve.

The new 'Every roadmap row references a real change-id' scenario says the change-id dir path is either 'openspec/changes/<change-id>/' or 'openspec/changes/archive/<date-prefix>-<change-id>/'. The <date-prefix> token is unspecified — archived changes in this repo use 'YYYY-MM-DD-' prefix (e.g., '2026-04-13-test-privacy-boundary/'), but the scenario doesn't state the format. A stricter reviewer tool couldn't enforce the convention without guessing.

The 'Non-phase changes are not required to have a row' scenario uses permissive negative language ('SHALL NOT be required to appear', 'if the author chooses to list it'). This is atypical for OpenSpec scenarios, which usually describe positive obligations under WHEN/THEN. As written, the scenario is hard to test — nothing can be verified because nothing is mandated.

Renumbering P1.5 bootstrap-fixes → P1.7 creates stale references in the just-archived openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-13-roadmap-v2-perplexity-integration/ artifacts (proposal.md, tasks.md, design.md, session-log.md all mention 'P1.5 bootstrap-fixes'). Archive convention is immutable — these can stay — but reviewers reading the archive later will find numbering that contradicts the current roadmap. No functional impact since change-ids (not P-numbers) are the identity source.

No automated detection for roadmap↔changes drift. If a future phase change is added under openspec/changes/<id>/ without a roadmap row, the spec violation is silent until a reviewer notices. Design D1 in the original roadmap-v2-perplexity-integration explicitly rejected CI enforcement as over-engineered at solo-developer scale, and this reconciliation inherits that choice.

The spec's 'Archived phase changes remain listed' scenario says the row SHALL have Status=archived and SHALL NOT be deleted — but doesn't constrain what the archive date annotation looks like. The roadmap rows here use '**archived** (YYYY-MM-DD)' in the Status column; no scenario mandates this format. A stricter reviewer couldn't detect a row that says only 'archived' with no date.

work-packages.yaml scope.deny includes 'openspec/specs/**', but the archive step — which happens after tasks 1.1–4.2 complete — writes to openspec/specs/tooling-roadmap/. The deny rule technically doesn't apply during archive (archive is outside the implementation-phase scope), but this is implicit; a strict scope-checker might flag the apparent contradiction. Note: this is also the blocker for Finding #1's resolution path (b).

The plan claims this change will replace the tooling-roadmap spec's `Purpose` placeholder (proposal.md:30-46, design.md:83-91, tasks.md:8-12,68-70), but the actual delta file contains only `## MODIFIED Requirements` and no non-requirement edit mechanism (openspec/changes/roadmap-v2-reconciliation/specs/tooling-roadmap/spec.md:3-59). The installed OpenSpec archive/apply path only parses top-level `ADDED/MODIFIED/REMOVED/RENAMED Requirements` blocks (`/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/@fission-ai/openspec/dist/core/parsers/requirement-blocks.js:80-102`) and rebuilds the target spec starting from `## Requirements`, preserving whatever is before that section (`.../core/specs-apply.js:59-63,156-246`). In other words, archiving this change will modify the requirement text but will not touch `openspec/specs/tooling-roadmap/spec.md:3-4`, so D5 silently fails even though `openspec validate --strict` passes.

The narrowed requirement still does not define `phase change` operationally enough to make the SHALL/MUST obligations testable. Design D4 says `sync-test-privacy-boundary-spec` is intentionally treated as a phase and that spec-sync listing is a "permission, not a mandate" (design.md:67-81), but the delta's non-phase scenario uses `updates an existing spec (spec-sync)` and `reconciles prior phase output` as its concrete cues for the non-phase bucket (openspec/changes/roadmap-v2-reconciliation/specs/tooling-roadmap/spec.md:32-41). That leaves future authors without a repo-readable rule for when a spec-sync/tooling/hygiene change is a required roadmap phase versus merely optional context, so the core invariant in the same requirement (`every phase change ... MUST appear as a row`, spec.md:16-20) remains partly judgment-based rather than consistently enforceable.

The renumbering does not appear to break live automation, because the roadmap itself says change-ids are the stable identity source (`openspec/roadmap.md:98-99`) and the active repo has no non-archive references to `P1.5 bootstrap-fixes` outside this reconciliation change. However, immutable archived artifacts from `roadmap-v2-perplexity-integration` still narrate the old numbering, e.g. `openspec/changes/archive/2026-04-13-roadmap-v2-perplexity-integration/session-log.md:19-22` and `.../tasks.md:92-94`. Future readers comparing those archives to the updated roadmap (`openspec/roadmap.md:30-32`) will see `bootstrap-fixes` referred to as both P1.5 and P1.7.

The plan's goal to replace the 'TBD' Purpose section in the tooling-roadmap spec (D5, task 1.1) is not reflected in the provided spec delta artifact at `openspec/changes/roadmap-v2-reconciliation/specs/tooling-roadmap/spec.md`. Even if the section were added to the delta, the OpenSpec `archive` tool typically only merges requirement-level ADDED/MODIFIED/REMOVED sections and lacks a mechanism to update the top-level Purpose field from a delta file. Without a manual edit task or a tool update, the Purpose will remain 'TBD' after archive.

The modified 'Roadmap Document Authoritative' requirement uses the term 'phase change' to delineate its scope ('Registry of phase changes'), but this term is not defined within the spec text. Design decision D1 accepts a reference-based definition ('a phase is what is in the table'), but this is tautological and doesn't provide future authors with an operational rule for deciding when a new change *must* be added to the roadmap vs. when it is *permitted* (D4).

The scenario 'Non-phase changes are not required to have a row' uses permissive, negative phrasing ('SHALL NOT be required to appear', 'if the author chooses'). Scenarios in OpenSpec are typically used to define testable, positive obligations under WHEN/THEN. As written, this scenario describes the absence of a requirement rather than a verifiable property of the system.

Renumbering P1.5 (bootstrap-fixes) to P1.7 creates a referential discrepancy with the just-archived `roadmap-v2-perplexity-integration` change artifacts, which multiple times cite 'P1.5 bootstrap-fixes'. While the proposal correctly notes that change-ids are the stable identity source, the shift may confuse future readers auditing the archive history against the current roadmap.

The scenario 'Every roadmap row references a real change-id' uses the token '<date-prefix>' but does not specify the required format (e.g. YYYY-MM-DD). The current project convention for archived changes is 'YYYY-MM-DD-<change-id>'. Without specifying the format, the scenario is insufficiently precise for automated validation tools.
